options.d: Move important files to backups before deleting originals

ci-copy-draft.title: Copy a draft into backups
ci-copy-draft.prompt:
Copy `inbox/draft.txt` to `backups/draft-copy.txt`.

Use the terminal. The editor is hidden on purpose for this task.
ci-copy-draft.hint: Use terminal commands such as `pwd`, `ls`, `mkdir -p`, `touch`, `cp`, `mv`, or `rm` depending on the task.

help.concept: This is a Linux terminal workspace task. Complete it by changing the file tree with terminal commands.
help.hint_1: Use `pwd` and `ls` to inspect where you are.
help.hint_2: The checker looks for the required folders/files and checks that forbidden files are gone.

ci-copy-draft.placeholder: Use the terminal to complete this task.

cp-copies.title: cp copies files
cp-copies.bodyMarkdown:
`cp` copies a file from one path to another.

```bash
cp notes.txt backups/notes.txt
```

The original remains in place, and the copy appears at the new path.

mv-moves-renames.title: mv moves or renames
mv-moves-renames.bodyMarkdown:
`mv` can move a file into a folder or rename it.

```bash
mv draft.txt final.txt
mv final.txt documents/final.txt
```

The old path disappears because the file was moved.

rm-safe-habits.title: rm deletes, so use it carefully
rm-safe-habits.bodyMarkdown:
`rm` removes files. It does not move them to a trash folder inside most terminal environments.

Safe habit:

```bash
ls
rm old-temp.txt
ls
```

Inspect before and after deletion.
